title: SonicWall Firewall Unused Access Rule Detection via Hit Counts (SonicWall)
dataSources: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall:firewall` with `rule`, `hits` or message-derived hit counters per access rule
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-30d@d
| where isnotnull(rule) AND match(lower(fw_action),"(?i)allow|accept|permit")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(hits) as daily_hits by rule, rule_name
| eventstats sum(daily_hits) as total_hits by rule
| where total_hits=0 OR total_hits<5
| dedup rule
| table rule, rule_name, total_hits
description: Many SonicWall policies accrue over years; zero-hit rules may be candidates for removal or indicate logging gaps. Tracking hits proves which controls are actually exercised.
value: Reduces attack surface and simplifies audits by retiring stale rules while validating that critical rules see traffic.
implementation: Enable enhanced firewall logging. If hit counts are not in structured fields, schedule periodic CLI/API exports into a lookup `sonicwall_rule_hits.csv` and join on rule number.
visualization: Bar chart (hits per rule), table (rule id, name, hits, last seen), scatter (rule age vs hits).

references.title: Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk
Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall reference

grandmaExplanation: Many SonicWall policies accrue over years; zero-hit rules may be candidates for removal or indicate logging gaps — we monitor SonicWall logs so teams can act before users feel pain.
